title: Memory Transfer Learning: How Memories are Transferred Across Domains in Coding Agents
authors: Kangsan Kim, Minki Kang, Taeil Kim, Yanlai Yang, Mengye Ren, Sung Ju Hwang
abstract: Memory-based self-evolution has emerged as a promising paradigm for coding agents. However, existing approaches typically restrict memory utilization to homogeneous task domains, failing to leverage the shared infrastructural foundations, such as runtime environments and programming languages, that exist across diverse real-world coding problems. To address this limitation, we investigate \textbf{Memory Transfer Learning} (MTL) by harnessing a unified memory pool from heterogeneous domains. We evaluate performance across 6 coding benchmarks using four memory representations, ranging from concrete traces to abstract insights. Our experiments demonstrate that cross-domain memory improves average performance by 3.7\%, primarily by transferring meta-knowledge, such as validation routines, rather than task-specific code. Importantly, we find that abstraction dictates transferability; high-level insights generalize well, whereas low-level traces often induce negative transfer due to excessive specificity. Furthermore, we show that transfer effectiveness scales with the size of the memory pool, and memory can be transferred even between different models. Our work establishes empirical design principles for expanding memory utilization beyond single-domain silos. Project page: https://memorytransfer.github.io/
title_zh: 记忆迁移学习：编码代理中记忆在不同领域间的传递方式
abstract_zh: 基于记忆的自我进化已成为编码智能体的一个有前景的范式。然而，现有方法通常将记忆利用限制在同质的任务领域内，未能充分利用跨多样化现实世界编码问题所共有的基础设施基础，例如运行时环境和编程语言。为解决这一局限性，我们通过从异构领域中整合统一的记忆池，探索了**记忆迁移学习**（Memory Transfer Learning, MTL）。我们在六个编码基准上，采用四种不同的记忆表示形式（从具体的执行轨迹到抽象的洞察）进行了评估。实验结果表明，跨域记忆可使平均性能提升3.7%，主要得益于元知识（如验证流程）的迁移，而非特定任务的代码。重要的是，我们发现抽象程度决定了记忆的可迁移性：高层级的洞察具有良好的泛化能力，而低层级的执行轨迹往往因过度具体化而导致负向迁移。此外，我们还发现迁移效果随记忆池规模的增大而提升，并且记忆可在不同模型之间实现有效传递。本研究确立了扩展记忆利用范围、突破单一领域封闭格局的实证设计原则。项目页面：https://memorytransfer.github.io/

title: AI-Assisted Peer Review at Scale: The AAAI-26 AI Review Pilot
authors: Joydeep Biswas, Sheila Schoepp, Gautham Vasan, Anthony Opipari, Arthur Zhang, Zichao Hu, Sebastian Joseph, Matthew Lease, Junyi Jessy Li, Peter Stone, Kiri L. Wagstaff, Matthew E. Taylor, Odest Chadwicke Jenkins
abstract: Scientific peer review faces mounting strain as submission volumes surge, making it increasingly difficult to sustain review quality, consistency, and timeliness. Recent advances in AI have led the community to consider its use in peer review, yet a key unresolved question is whether AI can generate technically sound reviews at real-world conference scale. Here we report the first large-scale field deployment of AI-assisted peer review: every main-track submission at AAAI-26 received one clearly identified AI review from a state-of-the-art system. The system combined frontier models, tool use, and safeguards in a multi-stage process to generate reviews for all 22,977 full-review papers in less than a day. A large-scale survey of AAAI-26 authors and program committee members showed that participants not only found AI reviews useful, but actually preferred them to human reviews on key dimensions such as technical accuracy and research suggestions. We also introduce a novel benchmark and find that our system substantially outperforms a simple LLM-generated review baseline at detecting a variety of scientific weaknesses. Together, these results show that state-of-the-art AI methods can already make meaningful contributions to scientific peer review at conference scale, opening a path toward the next generation of synergistic human-AI teaming for evaluating research.
title_zh: 大规模的AI辅助同行评审：AAAI-26 AI评审试点
abstract_zh: 随着投稿数量的激增，科学同行评审正面临日益加剧的压力，维持评审质量、一致性和及时性变得愈发困难。近年来，人工智能（AI）技术的进展促使学术界开始考虑将其应用于同行评审，但一个关键未解问题是：AI能否在真实会议规模下生成技术上严谨的评审意见？本文报告了首次大规模实地部署AI辅助同行评审：AAAI-26所有主会场投稿均获得了一篇由先进系统生成、明确标识的AI评审。该系统通过多阶段流程，结合前沿模型、工具使用和安全机制，在不到一天时间内为全部22,977篇完整论文生成了评审意见。对AAAI-26作者及程序委员会成员的大规模调查显示，参与者不仅认为AI评审具有实用价值，而且在技术准确性、研究建议等关键维度上，更倾向于选择AI评审而非人工评审。我们还引入了一个新型基准测试，发现该系统在识别多种科学缺陷方面显著优于简单的大型语言模型（LLM）生成的评审基线。综上所述，这些结果表明，当前最先进的AI方法已能在会议规模上对科学同行评审做出实质性贡献，为实现人机协同评估研究的下一代模式开辟了道路。

title: Towards Enabling An Artificial Self-Construction Software Life-cycle via Autopoietic Architectures
authors: Daniel Rodriguez-Cardenas, David Nader Palacio, Denys Poshyvanyk
abstract: Software engineering research has focused on automating maintenance and evolution processes to reduce costs and improve reliability. The emergence of foundation models (FMs) with strong code understanding and reasoning abilities offers new opportunities for autonomous software behavior. Inspired by Artificial Life (ALife), we propose a fundamental shift in the Software Development Life-Cycle (SDLC) by introducing self-construction mechanisms that enable software to evolve and maintain autonomously. This position paper explores the potential of Autopoietic Architectures, specifically Psi-Arch, as a foundational framework for self-constructing software. We first analyze the limitations of traditional maintenance approaches and identify gaps in current SDLC automation. Subsequently, we outline the core challenges in achieving self-construction, including the integration of foundation-model-based reasoning units and the establishment of novel architectural paradigms. Although this paper does not present a definitive solution, it seeks to catalyze discourse and inspire research toward a new paradigm in software engineering, one in which self-constructing software represents the next frontier in SDLC automation.
title_zh: 通过自产主义架构实现人工自我构建软件生命周期
abstract_zh: 软件工程研究一直致力于自动化维护与演化过程，以降低开发成本并提升系统可靠性。随着具备强大代码理解与推理能力的基础模型（Foundation Models, FMs）的出现，自主软件行为的实现迎来了新的机遇。受人工生命（Artificial Life, ALife）思想的启发，我们提出在软件开发生命周期（SDLC）中进行根本性转变，引入自构建机制，使软件能够自主演化与维护。本文作为一篇立场论文，探讨了自创生架构（Autopoietic Architectures），特别是Psi-Arch，作为自构建软件基础框架的潜力。首先，我们分析了传统维护方法的局限性，并指出现有SDLC自动化中存在的关键空白。随后，我们阐述了实现自构建所面临的核心挑战，包括基于基础模型的推理单元的集成，以及新型架构范式的建立。尽管本文并未提供确定性的解决方案，但其目的在于激发学术讨论，推动研究迈向软件工程的新范式——即以自构建软件为标志的SDLC自动化新前沿。

title: A Universal Textual Merge Strategy Based on Tokens for Version Control Systems
authors: Qiqi Jason Gu, Mikoláš Janota
abstract: Merging is a core operation in version control systems such as Git, but traditional line-based algorithms often yield spurious conflicts, particularly in the presence of refactorings or parallel edits. While syntax- and semantics-aware merging approaches can reduce conflicts, they introduce drawbacks such as loss of formatting, dependence on language-specific parsers, and limited flexibility across heterogeneous artifacts. To address this gap, we present Summer, a novel textual token-based merge algorithm independent of document formats. Dividing text into tokens, our approach formulates token-level changes in one branch into string-rewriting rules and move rules, and applies these rules to the text of the other branch to construct a merge. Despite being independent on programming languages, our move rules model extracting and inlining functions. We evaluated Summer on ConflictBench, a large benchmark of real-world merge scenarios, comparing it with five pioneering merge tools across Java and non-Java files. Experimental results show that Summer achieved the highest 36% accuracy in reproducing merges verbatim identical to developers', and ranked second in semantic accuracy.
title_zh: 基于标记的通用文本合并策略在版本控制系统中的应用
abstract_zh: 合并是Git等版本控制系统中的核心操作，但传统的基于行的算法常常会产生虚假冲突，尤其是在重构或并行编辑的情况下。尽管语法和语义感知的合并方法能够减少冲突，却也带来了格式丢失、依赖特定语言解析器以及在异构文件间灵活性受限等问题。为解决这一问题，我们提出了Summer——一种独立于文档格式的新型文本标记（token）级合并算法。该方法将文本划分为标记，将一个分支中的标记级变更表示为字符串重写规则和移动规则，并将这些规则应用于另一分支的文本以生成合并结果。尽管不依赖于具体编程语言，我们的移动规则仍能有效建模函数提取与内联操作。我们在ConflictBench——一个包含大量真实世界合并场景的大规模基准测试集上对Summer进行了评估，将其与五种具有代表性的合并工具在Java及非Java文件上的表现进行了对比。实验结果表明，Summer在重现开发者原始合并结果方面达到了36%的最高准确率，且在语义准确性方面排名第二。

title: Figma2Code: Automating Multimodal Design to Code in the Wild
authors: Yi Gui, Jiawan Zhang, Yina Wang, Tianran Ma, Yao Wan, Shilin He, Dongping Chen, Zhou Zhao, Wenbin Jiang, Xuanhua Shi, Hai Jin, Philip S Yu
abstract: Front-end development constitutes a substantial portion of software engineering, yet converting design mockups into production-ready User Interface (UI) code remains tedious and costly. While recent work has explored automating this process with Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), existing approaches typically rely solely on design images. As a result, they must infer complex UI details from images alone, often leading to degraded results. In real-world development workflows, however, design mockups are usually delivered as Figma files, a widely used tool for front-end design, that embed rich multimodal information (e.g., metadata and assets) essential for generating high-quality UI. To bridge this gap, we introduce Figma2Code, a new task that advances design-to-code into a multimodal setting and aims to automate design-to-code in the wild. Specifically, we collect paired design images and their corresponding metadata files from the Figma community. We then apply a series of processing operations, including rule-based filtering, human- and MLLM-based annotation and screening, and metadata refinement. This process yields 3,055 samples, from which designers curate a balanced dataset of 213 high-quality cases. Using this dataset, we benchmark ten state-of-the-art open-source and proprietary MLLMs. Our results show that while proprietary models achieve superior visual fidelity, they remain limited in layout responsiveness and code maintainability. Further experiments across modalities and ablation studies corroborate this limitation, partly due to models' tendency to directly map primitive visual attributes from Figma metadata.
title_zh: Figma2Code：在真实场景中实现多模态设计到代码的自动化
abstract_zh: 前端开发构成了软件工程中的重要部分，然而将设计原型图转换为可投入生产的用户界面（UI）代码仍然是一项繁琐且成本高昂的工作。尽管近期研究尝试利用多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）来自动化这一过程，但现有方法通常仅依赖设计图像，因此必须仅从图像中推断复杂的UI细节，常常导致生成结果质量下降。在实际的开发工作流中，设计原型通常以Figma文件的形式交付——Figma是前端设计领域广泛使用的工具，其文件中嵌入了丰富的多模态信息（如元数据和资源资产），这些信息对于生成高质量的UI至关重要。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了Figma2Code这一新任务，将设计到代码的转换推进至多模态场景，旨在实现真实环境下的自动化设计转代码。具体而言，我们从Figma社区收集了成对的设计图像及其对应的元数据文件，并通过一系列处理操作，包括基于规则的筛选、人工与MLLM结合的标注与筛选，以及元数据的优化处理，最终获得3,055个样本。在此基础上，设计师从中精心挑选出213个高质量、具有代表性的案例，构建了一个平衡的数据集。基于该数据集，我们对十种最先进的开源及专有MLLMs进行了基准测试。实验结果表明，虽然专有模型在视觉保真度方面表现更优，但在布局响应性和代码可维护性方面仍存在明显局限。跨模态实验与消融研究进一步验证了这一缺陷，部分原因在于模型倾向于直接将Figma元数据中的基础视觉属性映射到输出代码中。

title: V2E: Validating Smart Contract Vulnerabilities through Profit-driven Exploit Generation and Execution
authors: Jingwen Zhang, Yuhong Nan, Kaiwen Ning, Mingxi Ye, Wei Li, Yuming Xiao, Yuming Feng, Weizhe Zhang, Zibin Zheng
abstract: Smart contracts are a critical component of blockchain systems. Due to the large amount of digital assets carried by smart contracts, their security is of critical importance. Although numerous tools have been developed for detecting smart contract vulnerability, their effectiveness remains limited, particularly due to the high false positives included in the reported results. Therefore, developers and auditors are often overwhelmed with manually verifying the reported issues. A fundamental reason behind this is that while a reported vulnerability satisfies specific vulnerable patterns, it may not actually be exploitable, either because the vulnerable code cannot be triggered or it does not result in any financial loss. In this paper, we propose V2E, a new framework for validating whether a reported vulnerability is truly exploitable. The core idea of V2E is to automatically generate executable Proof-of-Concept Exploit (PoC for short), and then assess if the vulnerability could be triggered and incur any real damage (i.e., causing financial loss) by the PoC. While LLMs have shown proficiency in PoC generation, achieving our task is by no means trivial. In detail, it is difficult for LLM to: (1) generate and update PoC to trigger a specific vulnerability, (2) evaluate the PoC's effectiveness to validate exploitable vulnerability. To this end, V2E automates the whole process through a novel combination of PoC generation, validation, and refinement: (1) Firstly, V2E generates targeted PoCs by analyzing potential vulnerability paths. (2) Then, V2E verifies the validity of PoCs through triggerability and profitability analysis. (3) In addition, V2E iteratively refines the generated PoC based on PoC execution feedback, therefore, increasing the chance to confirm the vulnerability. Evaluation on 264 manually labeled contracts shows that V2E outperforms the baseline approach.
title_zh: V2E：通过利润驱动的漏洞利用生成与执行来验证智能合约漏洞
abstract_zh: 智能合约是区块链系统中的关键组成部分。由于智能合约承载着大量数字资产，其安全性至关重要。尽管已开发出众多用于检测智能合约漏洞的工具，但它们的有效性仍然有限，尤其是报告结果中包含大量误报。因此，开发者和审计人员常常需要手动验证大量报告问题，负担沉重。造成这一现象的根本原因在于：即使报告的漏洞符合特定的脆弱模式，也可能实际上无法被利用，要么是因为脆弱代码无法被触发，要么是因为它不会导致任何经济损失。本文提出了一种名为V2E的新框架，用于验证报告的漏洞是否真正可被利用。V2E的核心思想是：自动生成可执行的漏洞概念验证（PoC），然后通过该PoC评估漏洞是否能够被触发并造成实际损害（即引发财务损失）。虽然大语言模型（LLMs）在生成PoC方面已表现出一定能力，但要完成我们的任务仍极具挑战性。具体而言，LLM面临以下困难：（1）难以生成并持续更新PoC以成功触发特定漏洞；（2）难以有效评估PoC的实际效果，从而验证漏洞是否可被利用。为此，V2E通过一种创新的PoC生成、验证与优化相结合的方法，实现了整个流程的自动化：（1）首先，V2E通过分析潜在的漏洞路径，生成针对性的PoC；（2）随后，V2E通过可触发性与盈利性分析来验证PoC的有效性；（3）此外，V2E根据PoC执行反馈进行迭代优化，从而提高确认漏洞真实性的概率。在264个经人工标注的合约上的评估表明，V2E在性能上显著优于基线方法。

title: Synthesizing Instruction-Tuning Datasets with Contrastive Decoding
authors: Tatsuya Ichinose, Youmi Ma, Masanari Oi, Ryuto Koike, Naoaki Okazaki
abstract: Using responses generated by high-performing large language models (LLMs) for instruction tuning has become a widely adopted approach. However, the existing literature overlooks a property of LLM-generated responses: they conflate world knowledge acquired during pre-training with instruction-following capabilities acquired during post-training. We hypothesize that disentangling the instruction-following capabilities from pre-trained knowledge improves the effectiveness of instruction tuning. To this end, we propose CoDIT, a method that applies contrastive decoding between a post-trained model and its pre-trained counterpart during response generation. The method suppresses pre-trained knowledge shared between the two models while amplifying the instruction-following behavior acquired via post-training, resulting in responses that more purely reflect instruction-following capabilities. Experiment results demonstrate that models trained on datasets constructed via CoDIT consistently outperform those trained on directly generated responses. Training on our datasets also yields better performance than on existing publicly available instruction-tuning datasets across multiple benchmarks. Furthermore, we theoretically and empirically show that CoDIT can be interpreted as distilling the chat vector from parameter space to text space, enabling the transfer of instruction-tuning capabilities across models of different architectures.
title_zh: 使用对比解码合成指令微调数据集
abstract_zh: 利用高性能大语言模型（LLMs）生成的响应进行指令微调，已成为一种广泛采用的方法。然而，现有文献忽视了LLM生成响应的一个关键特性：其同时融合了预训练阶段获取的世界知识与后训练阶段习得的指令遵循能力。我们假设，将指令遵循能力从预训练知识中解耦，能够提升指令微调的效果。为此，我们提出CoDIT方法，该方法在生成响应时，对后训练模型与其对应的预训练模型进行对比解码。该方法抑制了两个模型间共享的预训练知识，同时增强通过后训练获得的指令遵循行为，从而生成更纯粹反映指令遵循能力的响应。实验结果表明，基于CoDIT构建的数据集进行训练的模型，在多个任务上均显著优于使用直接生成响应训练的模型。此外，使用我们的数据集进行训练，性能也优于现有的公开可用指令微调数据集，在多个基准测试中表现更优。进一步地，我们从理论和实证层面证明，CoDIT可被解释为将参数空间中的“对话向量”蒸馏到文本空间，从而实现不同架构模型之间指令微调能力的迁移。

title: Don't Let AI Agents YOLO Your Files: Shifting Information and Control to Filesystems for Agent Safety and Autonomy
authors: Shawn, Zhong, Junxuan Liao, Jing Liu, Mai Zheng, Andrea C. Arpaci-Dusseau, Remzi H. Arpaci-Dusseau
abstract: AI coding agents operate directly on users' filesystems, where they regularly corrupt data, delete files, and leak secrets. Current approaches force a tradeoff between safety and autonomy: unrestricted access risks harm, while frequent permission prompts burden users and block agents. To understand this problem, we conduct the first systematic study of agent filesystem misuse, analyzing 290 public reports across 13 frameworks. Our analysis reveals that today's agents have limited information about their filesystem effects and insufficient control over them. We therefore argue for shifting this information and control to the filesystem itself. Based on this principle, we design YoloFS, an agent-native filesystem with three techniques. Staging isolates all mutations before commit, giving users corrective control. Snapshots extend this control to agents, letting them detect and correct their own mistakes. Progressive permission provides users with preventive control by gating access with minimal interaction. To evaluate YoloFS, we introduce a new methodology that captures user-agent-filesystem interactions. On 11 tasks with hidden side effects, YoloFS enables agent self-correction in 8 while keeping all effects staged and reviewable. On 112 routine tasks, YoloFS requires fewer user interactions while matching the baseline success rate.
title_zh: 别让AI代理随意“乱来”你的文件：将信息和控制权转移至文件系统，以保障代理的安全与自主性
abstract_zh: AI 编码代理直接操作用户的文件系统，频繁导致数据损坏、文件删除以及机密信息泄露。当前的方法在安全性和自主性之间被迫做出权衡：无限制的访问存在风险，而频繁的权限提示又会给用户带来负担并阻碍代理的运行。为深入理解这一问题，我们开展了首次针对代理文件系统误用的系统性研究，分析了来自13个框架的290份公开报告。我们的分析表明，当前的代理对自身文件系统操作的影响了解有限，且控制能力不足。因此，我们主张将这种信息和控制权交还给文件系统本身。基于这一理念，我们设计了 YoloFS——一种面向代理的原生文件系统，包含三项核心技术：暂存机制在提交前隔离所有修改，使用户能够进行纠正；快照功能将这种控制扩展至代理自身，使其能够检测并修正自身的错误；渐进式权限则通过最少的交互实现对访问的预防性控制，赋予用户主动干预的能力。为评估 YoloFS，我们提出了一种新的方法论，用于捕捉用户、代理与文件系统之间的交互过程。在11项存在隐藏副作用的任务中，YoloFS 能够实现8次代理自我纠错，同时确保所有操作均处于暂存状态，可供审查；在112项常规任务中，YoloFS 在保持基线成功率的同时，显著减少了用户交互次数。

title: From Exploration to Specification: LLM-Based Property Generation for Mobile App Testing
authors: Yiheng Xiong, Shiwen Song, Bo Ma, Ting Su, Xiaofei Xie
abstract: Mobile apps often suffer from functional bugs that do not cause crashes but instead manifest as incorrect behaviors under specific user interactions. Such bugs are difficult to detect automatically because they often lack explicit test oracles. Property-based testing can effectively expose them by checking intended behavioral properties under diverse interactions. However, its use largely depends on manually written properties, whose construction is difficult and expensive, limiting its practical use for mobile apps. To address this limitation, we propose PropGen, an automated approach for generating properties for Android apps. However, this task is challenging for two reasons: app functionalities are often hard to systematically uncover and execute, and properties are difficult to derive accurately from observed behaviors. To this end, PropGen performs functionality-guided exploration to collect behavioral evidence from app executions, synthesizes properties from the collected evidence, and refines imprecise properties based on testing feedback. We implemented PropGen and evaluated it on 12 real-world Android apps. The results show that PropGen can effectively identify and execute valid app functionalities, generate valid properties, and repair most imprecise ones. Across all apps, PropGen identified 1,210 valid functionalities and correctly executed 977 of them, compared with 491 and 187 for the baseline. It generated 985 properties, 912 of which were valid, and repaired 118 of 127 imprecise ones exposed during testing. With the resulting properties, we found 25 previously unknown functional bugs in the latest versions of the subject apps, many of which were missed by existing functional testing techniques.
title_zh: 从探索到规范：基于大语言模型的移动应用测试属性生成
abstract_zh: 移动应用常常存在一些不会导致崩溃但会在特定用户操作下表现出错误行为的功能性缺陷。这类缺陷难以通过自动化手段检测，因为它们通常缺乏明确的测试断言（test oracle）。属性测试（property-based testing）能够有效发现这些缺陷，通过在多样化的用户交互下验证应用应有的行为属性。然而，其应用在很大程度上依赖于人工编写的属性，而属性的构建既困难又昂贵，限制了其在移动应用中的实际使用。为解决这一局限，我们提出了 PropGen——一种针对 Android 应用自动生成属性的自动化方法。然而，该任务面临两大挑战：一是应用功能往往难以系统地发现和执行；二是从观察到的行为中准确推导出属性十分困难。为此，PropGen 采用功能引导式探索（functionality-guided exploration），从应用运行中收集行为证据，基于这些证据合成属性，并根据测试反馈对不精确的属性进行修正。我们实现了 PropGen，并在 12 个真实世界的 Android 应用上进行了评估。结果表明，PropGen 能够有效识别并执行有效的应用功能，生成有效的属性，并修复大多数不精确的属性。在所有被测应用中，PropGen 共识别出 1,210 项有效功能，其中正确执行了 977 项，而基线方法仅识别出 491 项，正确执行 187 项。PropGen 生成了 985 条属性，其中 912 条为有效属性，并修复了测试中暴露的 127 个不精确属性中的 118 个。利用生成的属性，我们在目标应用的最新版本中发现了 25 个此前未知的功能性缺陷，其中许多缺陷被现有的功能测试技术所遗漏。

title: From $P(y|x)$ to $P(y)$: Investigating Reinforcement Learning in Pre-train Space
authors: Yuqiao Tan, Minzheng Wang, Bo Liu, Zichen Liu, Tian Liang, Shizhu He, Jun Zhao, Kang Liu
abstract: While reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) significantly enhances LLM reasoning by optimizing the conditional distribution P(y|x), its potential is fundamentally bounded by the base model's existing output distribution. Optimizing the marginal distribution P(y) in the Pre-train Space addresses this bottleneck by encoding reasoning ability and preserving broad exploration capacity. Yet, conventional pre-training relies on static corpora for passive learning, leading to a distribution shift that hinders targeted reasoning enhancement. In this paper, we introduce PreRL (Pre-train Space RL), which applies reward-driven online updates directly to P(y). We theoretically and empirically validate the strong gradient alignment between log P(y) and log P(y|x), establishing PreRL as a viable surrogate for standard RL. Furthermore, we uncover a critical mechanism: Negative Sample Reinforcement (NSR) within PreRL serves as an exceptionally effective driver for reasoning. NSR-PreRL rapidly prunes incorrect reasoning spaces while stimulating endogenous reflective behaviors, increasing transition and reflection thoughts by 14.89x and 6.54x, respectively. Leveraging these insights, we propose Dual Space RL (DSRL), a Policy Reincarnation strategy that initializes models with NSR-PreRL to expand the reasoning horizon before transitioning to standard RL for fine-grained optimization. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DSRL consistently outperforms strong baselines, proving that pre-train space pruning effectively steers the policy toward a refined correct reasoning subspace.
title_zh: 从 $P(y|x)$ 到 $P(y)$：探索预训练空间中的强化学习
abstract_zh: 尽管基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）通过优化条件分布 $P(y|x)$ 显著提升了大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力，但其潜力从根本上受限于基础模型现有的输出分布。通过在预训练空间中优化边缘分布 $P(y)$，可以突破这一瓶颈，实现推理能力的编码并保持广泛的探索能力。然而，传统的预训练依赖静态语料库进行被动学习，导致分布偏移，阻碍了针对性推理能力的提升。本文提出 PreRL（预训练空间强化学习），直接对 $P(y)$ 进行基于奖励的在线更新。我们从理论和实证两方面验证了 $\log P(y)$ 与 $\log P(y|x)$ 之间存在强烈的梯度一致性，确立了 PreRL 作为标准强化学习的有效替代方案。此外，我们发现一个关键机制：PreRL 中的负样本强化（NSR）成为推动推理能力发展的极高效驱动力。NSR-PreRL 能快速剔除错误的推理空间，同时激发内在的反思行为，使状态转移和反思性思考分别提升了 14.89 倍和 6.54 倍。基于这些发现，我们提出双空间强化学习（DSRL），一种“策略重生”策略：先利用 NSR-PreRL 初始化模型以拓展推理范围，再转入标准强化学习进行精细化优化。大量实验表明，DSRL 持续优于多个强基线模型，证明预训练空间中的有效剪枝能够有效引导策略向更精确的正确推理子空间演进。

title: From Feelings to Metrics: Understanding and Formalizing How Users Vibe-Test LLMs
authors: Itay Itzhak, Eliya Habba, Gabriel Stanovsky, Yonatan Belinkov
abstract: Evaluating LLMs is challenging, as benchmark scores often fail to capture models' real-world usefulness. Instead, users often rely on ``vibe-testing'': informal experience-based evaluation, such as comparing models on coding tasks related to their own workflow. While prevalent, vibe-testing is often too ad hoc and unstructured to analyze or reproduce at scale. In this work, we study how vibe-testing works in practice and then formalize it to support systematic analysis. We first analyze two empirical resources: (1) a survey of user evaluation practices, and (2) a collection of in-the-wild model comparison reports from blogs and social media. Based on these resources, we formalize vibe-testing as a two-part process: users personalize both what they test and how they judge responses. We then introduce a proof-of-concept evaluation pipeline that follows this formulation by generating personalized prompts and comparing model outputs using user-aware subjective criteria. In experiments on coding benchmarks, we find that combining personalized prompts and user-aware evaluation can change which model is preferred, reflecting the role of vibe-testing in practice. These findings suggest that formalized vibe-testing can serve as a useful approach for bridging benchmark scores and real-world experience.
title_zh: 从感受到度量：理解并形式化用户如何“ vibe 测试”大语言模型
abstract_zh: 评估大型语言模型（LLMs）具有挑战性，因为基准测试分数往往无法反映模型在实际应用中的真正价值。相反，用户通常依赖“直觉测试”——一种基于经验的非正式评估方式，例如将模型在与其自身工作流程相关的编码任务中进行比较。尽管这种做法十分普遍，但其往往过于随意且缺乏结构，难以进行系统化分析或大规模复现。在本研究中，我们深入探讨了直觉测试在实践中的运作机制，并将其形式化以支持系统的分析。我们首先分析了两项实证资源：（1）关于用户评估实践的调查，以及（2）来自博客和社交媒体的野外模型对比报告集合。基于这些资源，我们将直觉测试形式化为一个两阶段过程：用户既个性化地选择测试内容，也个性化地制定评价标准。随后，我们提出一个概念验证评估流程，该流程遵循上述形式化框架，通过生成个性化提示，并使用用户感知的主观标准来比较模型输出。在编码基准测试的实验中，我们发现结合个性化提示与用户感知评估方法，可能会改变用户对模型的偏好，这反映了直觉测试在实际中的作用。这些发现表明，形式化的直觉测试可作为一种有效手段，弥合基准测试得分与真实用户体验之间的差距。

title: TREX: Automating LLM Fine-tuning via Agent-Driven Tree-based Exploration
authors: Zerun Ma, Guoqiang Wang, Xinchen Xie, Yicheng Chen, He Du, Bowen Li, Yanan Sun, Wenran Liu, Kai Chen, Yining Li
abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have empowered AI research agents to perform isolated scientific tasks, automating complex, real-world workflows, such as LLM training, remains a significant challenge. In this paper, we introduce TREX, a multi-agent system that automates the entire LLM training life-cycle. By orchestrating collaboration between two core modules-the Researcher and the Executor-the system seamlessly performs requirement analysis, open-domain literature and data research, formulation of training strategies, preparation of data recipes, and model training and evaluation. The multi-round experimental process is modeled as a search tree, enabling the system to efficiently plan exploration paths, reuse historical results, and distill high-level insights from iterative trials. To evaluate the capability of automated LLM training, we construct FT-Bench, a benchmark comprising 10 tasks derived from real-world scenarios, ranging from optimizing fundamental model capabilities to enhancing performance on domain-specific tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that the TREX agent consistently optimizes model performance on target tasks.
title_zh: TREX：通过代理驱动的树状探索实现大模型微调的自动化
abstract_zh: 尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）已使AI研究代理能够执行孤立的科学任务，但自动化复杂且现实世界中的工作流程（如LLM训练）仍面临重大挑战。本文提出TREX，一个用于自动化整个LLM训练生命周期的多智能体系统。通过协调两个核心模块——研究者（Researcher）与执行者（Executor）之间的协作，该系统可无缝完成需求分析、开放域文献与数据调研、训练策略制定、数据配方准备，以及模型训练与评估等任务。多轮实验过程被建模为搜索树结构，使系统能够高效规划探索路径，复用历史结果，并从迭代试验中提炼出高层次洞察。为评估自动化LLM训练的能力，我们构建了FT-Bench基准测试集，包含10个源自真实场景的任务，涵盖从优化基础模型能力到提升特定领域任务性能等多个方面。实验结果表明，TREX智能体在目标任务上始终能有效优化模型性能。

title: AI Coding Agents Need Better Compiler Remarks
authors: Akash Deo, Simone Campanoni, Tommy McMichen
abstract: Modern AI agents optimize programs by refactoring source code to trigger trusted compiler transformations. This preserves program semantics and reduces source code pollution, making the program easier to maintain and portable across architectures. However, this collaborative workflow is limited by legacy compiler interfaces, which obscure analysis behind unstructured, lossy optimization remarks that have been designed for human intuition rather than machine logic. Using the TSVC benchmark, we evaluate the efficacy of existing optimization feedback. We find that while precise remarks provide actionable feedback (3.3x success rate), ambiguous remarks are actively detrimental, triggering semantic-breaking hallucinations. By replacing ambiguous remarks with precise ones, we show that structured, precise analysis information unlocks the capabilities of small models, proving that the bottleneck is the interface, not the agent. We conclude that future compilers must expose structured, actionable feedback designed specifically for the future of autonomous performance engineering.
title_zh: AI 编码代理需要更出色的编译器注释
abstract_zh: 现代AI代理通过重构源代码以触发可信编译器的优化转换来提升程序性能。这种方法保持了程序语义的完整性，减少了源代码污染，使程序更易于维护，并在不同架构间具有更好的可移植性。然而，这种协作式工作流程受限于传统编译器接口——这些接口将分析信息隐藏在非结构化、有损的优化注释中，其设计初衷是服务于人类直觉，而非机器逻辑。我们使用TSVC基准测试评估了现有优化反馈的有效性，发现虽然精确的注释能提供可操作的反馈（成功率提升3.3倍），但模糊的注释反而具有破坏性，会引发语义错误的“幻觉”。通过将模糊注释替换为精确注释，我们证明：结构化且精确的分析信息能够释放小型模型的潜力，从而表明瓶颈在于接口本身，而非AI代理的能力。因此，我们得出结论：未来的编译器必须提供专为自主性能工程设计的结构化、可操作的反馈机制。

title: DiPO: Disentangled Perplexity Policy Optimization for Fine-grained Exploration-Exploitation Trade-Off
authors: Xiaofan Li, Ming Yang, Zhiyuan Ma, Shichao Ma, Jintao Du, Yu Cheng, Weiqiang Wang, Zhizhong Zhang, Xin Tan, Yanyun Qu, Lizhuang Ma, Yuan Xie
abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has catalyzed significant advances in the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, effectively managing the exploration and exploitation trade-off remains a critical challenge. In this paper, we fully analyze the exploration and exploitation dilemma of extremely hard and easy samples during the training and propose a new fine-grained trade-off mechanism. Concretely, we introduce a perplexity space disentangling strategy that divides the sample space into distinct exploration (high perplexity) and exploitation (low perplexity) subspaces, thereby mining fine-grained samples requiring exploration-exploitation trade-off. Subsequently, we propose a bidirectional reward allocation mechanism with a minimum impact on verification rewards to implement perplexity-guided exploration and exploitation, enabling more stable policy optimization. Finally, we have evaluated our method on two mainstream tasks: mathematical reasoning and function calling, and experimental results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method, confirming its effectiveness in enhancing LLM performance by fine-grained exploration-exploitation trade-off.
title_zh: DiPO：解耦困惑度策略优化用于细粒度的探索-利用权衡
abstract_zh: 基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）已显著推动了大语言模型（LLMs）推理能力的发展。然而，如何有效管理探索与利用之间的权衡仍是关键挑战。本文全面分析了在训练过程中极端难样本与简单样本所面临的探索-利用困境，并提出一种新的细粒度权衡机制。具体而言，我们引入了一种困惑度空间解耦策略，将样本空间划分为不同的探索子空间（高困惑度）和利用子空间（低困惑度），从而挖掘出需要精细探索与利用权衡的细粒度样本。随后，我们设计了一种双向奖励分配机制，在最小影响验证奖励的前提下，实现基于困惑度引导的探索与利用，进而促进更稳定的策略优化。最后，我们在两个主流任务——数学推理与函数调用上对所提方法进行了评估，实验结果表明该方法具有显著优势，验证了通过细粒度探索-利用权衡提升LLM性能的有效性。

title: SFT-GRPO Data Overlap as a Post-Training Hyperparameter for Autoformalization
authors: Xiaole Su, Kasey Zhang, Andy Lyu
abstract: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) followed by Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is a common post-training recipe. We conduct a controlled ablation over SFT-GRPO data overlap, evaluating Qwen3-8B (thinking disabled) post-trained for Lean 4 autoformalization under six conditions that differ solely in training recipe: a base model, SFT-only, GRPO-only, and three SFT+GRPO configurations where 0 percent, 30 percent, or 100 percent of the GRPO prompts coincide with the SFT corpus. Keeping SFT and GRPO data disjoint consistently outperforms full overlap at zero additional compute cost. Evaluating on Gaokao-Formal and PutnamBench under both compile pass at k and semantic pass at k assessed by an LLM judge, we find that lower overlap is monotonically associated with higher compilation and semantic accuracy. At 0 percent overlap, GRPO yields a 10.4 percentage point semantic gain over SFT alone on Gaokao, while at 100 percent overlap both metrics remain flat, rendering the GRPO stage effectively redundant. We further show that dual-metric evaluation reveals compile semantic gaps exceeding 30 percentage points for the highest compiling models, a disparity invisible under compile-only benchmarking. To our knowledge, this is the first controlled investigation of SFT-GRPO data overlap as a post-training hyperparameter, demonstrating how model behavior varies based on the degree of data sharing between training stages.
title_zh: SFT-GRPO 数据重叠作为自动形式化后训练的超参数
abstract_zh: 监督微调（SFT）后接组相对策略优化（GRPO）是一种常见的后续训练方法。我们对SFT-GRPO数据重叠程度进行了受控消融实验，评估了在Lean 4自动形式化任务上经过后训练的Qwen3-8B模型（关闭思维功能），共设置了六种仅在训练方案上有所不同的条件：基础模型、仅SFT、仅GRPO，以及三种SFT+GRPO配置，其中GRPO提示与SFT语料库的重合比例分别为0%、30%和100%。保持SFT与GRPO数据完全分离的方案，在不增加任何计算成本的情况下，始终优于完全重叠的情况。在Gaokao-Formal和PutnamBench两个基准上，分别采用LLM裁判评估的编译通过率（compile pass at k）和语义通过率（semantic pass at k），我们发现较低的数据重叠度与更高的编译准确率及语义准确率呈单调正相关。在0%重叠的情况下，GRPO相较于仅使用SFT在Gaokao任务上实现了10.4个百分点的语义性能提升；而在100%重叠时，两项指标均无明显变化，表明GRPO阶段实际上已变得冗余。此外，我们还发现双指标评估揭示出，对于编译成功率最高的模型，其编译与语义之间的差距超过30个百分点，这一差异在仅关注编译表现的评估中是无法察觉的。据我们所知，这是首次对SFT-GRPO数据重叠作为后训练超参数进行的受控研究，展示了模型行为如何随不同训练阶段间数据共享程度的变化而演变。

title: Persistent Iterators with Value Semantics
authors: Yihe Li, Gregory J. Duck
abstract: Iterators are a fundamental programming abstraction for traversing and modifying elements in containers in mainstream imperative languages such as C++. Iterators provide a uniform access mechanism that hides low-level implementation details of the underlying data structure. However, iterators over mutable containers suffer from well-known hazards including invalidation, aliasing, data races, and subtle side effects. Immutable data structures, as used in functional programming languages, avoid the pitfalls of mutation but rely on a very different programming model based on recursion and higher-order combinators rather than iteration. However, these combinators are not always well-suited to expressing certain algorithms, and recursion can expose implementation details of the underlying data structure. In this paper, we propose persistent iterators -- a new abstraction that reconciles the familiar iterator-based programming style of imperative languages with the semantics of persistent data structures. A persistent iterator snapshots the version of its underlying container at creation, ensuring safety against invalidation and aliasing. Iterator operations operate on the iterator-local copy of the container, giving true value semantics: variables can be rebound to new persistent values while previous versions remain accessible. We implement our approach in the form of LibFPP -- a C++ container library providing persistent vectors, maps, sets, strings, and other abstractions as persistent counterparts to the Standard Template Library (STL). Our evaluation shows that LibFPP retains the expressiveness of iterator-based programming, eliminates iterator-invalidation, and achieves asymptotic complexities comparable to STL implementations. Our design targets use cases where persistence and safety are desired, while allowing developers to retain familiar iterator-based programming patterns.
title_zh: 具有值语义的持久迭代器
abstract_zh:
迭代器是主流命令式编程语言（如C++）中遍历和修改容器元素的基本抽象。它们提供了一种统一的访问机制，隐藏了底层数据结构的低层实现细节。然而，对可变容器的迭代器存在众所周知的风险，包括失效、别名、数据竞争以及微妙的副作用。函数式编程语言中使用的不可变数据结构虽然避免了这些缺陷，但依赖于一种截然不同的编程模型——基于递归和高阶组合子，而非迭代。然而，这些组合子并不总是适合表达某些算法，而递归又可能暴露底层数据结构的实现细节。

在本文中，我们提出了**持久化迭代器**（persistent iterators）这一新抽象，旨在调和命令式语言中熟悉的基于迭代器的编程风格与持久化数据结构的语义。持久化迭代器在创建时对底层容器的版本进行快照，从而确保对失效和别名的安全性。迭代器操作作用于迭代器本地的容器副本，实现了真正的值语义：变量可以重新绑定到新的持久化值，同时旧版本仍可访问。

我们以LibFPP的形式实现了该方法——一个C++容器库，提供了持久化向量、映射、集合、字符串及其他抽象，作为标准模板库（STL）的持久化对应物。我们的评估表明，LibFPP保持了基于迭代器编程的表达能力，消除了迭代器失效问题，并达到了与STL实现相当的渐近复杂度。该设计适用于需要持久性和安全性的场景，同时允许开发者保留熟悉的基于迭代器的编程模式。

title: ATLAAS: Automatic Tensor-Level Abstraction of Accelerator Semantics
authors: Ruijie Gao, Haoran Jin, Jirong Yang, Nathaniel Bleier
abstract: Numerous tensor accelerator designs have been proposed, yet most lack well-documented ISAs and compiler backends, limiting evaluation to a handful of operators. Recent work has shown that given a tensor-level ISA specification, complete software stacks including compiler backends can be automatically generated--but writing such specifications remains a manual, expert-driven process. We present ATLAAS, the first end-to-end MLIR-based pipeline that lifts RTL-extracted accelerator semantics to tensor ISA specifications. Starting from bit-level LLVM IR produced by prior architecture-level model extraction, ATLAAS applies an 8-pass semantic lifting pipeline that progressively recovers high-level tensor structure--MAC idioms, saturation semantics, multi-dimensional buffer organizations, and data layout transformations--emitting specifications that immediately enable automatic software stack generation through the ACT ecosystem. We evaluate ATLAAS on the Gemmini systolic-array accelerator, where the pipeline collapses bit-level MLIR by up to 92.9% on processing elements and 24-34% on controller modules. ATLAAS discovers hardware features omitted from the hand-written reference, with correctness validated via Z3 SMT equivalence proofs. Generality is confirmed on TVM's VTA processor, where the same pipeline lifts all four datapath modules without accelerator-specific changes, enabling an automated path from RTL to a performance-competitive compiler backend.
title_zh: ATLAAS：加速器语义的自动张量级抽象
abstract_zh: 已有大量张量加速器设计被提出，但大多数缺乏详尽文档化的指令集架构（ISA）和编译器后端，导致其评估仅限于少数算子。近期研究显示，若提供张量级ISA规范，可自动生成完整的软件栈（包括编译器后端），但编写此类规范仍需人工完成且依赖专家经验。本文提出ATLAAS——首个基于MLIR的端到端流水线，能够将从RTL中提取的加速器语义提升至张量ISA规范。该方法从先前架构级模型提取所生成的比特级LLVM IR出发，通过一个八阶段语义提升流程，逐步恢复高层张量结构——包括MAC操作模式、饱和语义、多维缓冲组织以及数据布局变换等特性，最终输出的规范可立即通过ACT生态系统实现自动软件栈生成。我们在Gemmini列阵加速器上对ATLAAS进行了评估，结果显示该流水线在处理单元上将比特级MLIR规模压缩高达92.9%，在控制器模块上压缩24%–34%。ATLAAS还发现了手工编写参考规范中遗漏的硬件特性，并通过Z3 SMT等价性证明验证了其正确性。在TVM的VTA处理器上的实验进一步验证了其通用性：相同流水线无需针对特定加速器进行修改，即可成功提升全部四个数据通路模块，从而实现了从RTL到性能具备竞争力的编译器后端的自动化路径。

title: ToolOmni: Enabling Open-World Tool Use via Agentic learning with Proactive Retrieval and Grounded Execution
authors: Shouzheng Huang, Meishan Zhang, Baotian Hu, Min Zhang
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) enhance their problem-solving capability by utilizing external tools. However, in open-world scenarios with massive and evolving tool repositories, existing methods relying on static embedding retrieval or parameter memorization of tools struggle to align user intent with tool semantics or generalize to unseen tools, respectively, leading to suboptimal accuracy of open-world tool retrieval and execution. To address these, we present ToolOmni, a unified agentic framework that enables LLMs for open-world tool use by proactive retrieval and grounded execution within a reasoning loop. First, we construct a cold-start multi-turn interaction dataset to instill foundational agentic capabilities via Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT). Then, we introduce open-world tool learning based on a Decoupled Multi-Objective GRPO algorithm, which simultaneously optimizes LLMs for both tool retrieval accuracy and execution efficacy in online environments. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ToolOmni achieves state-of-the-art performance both in retrieval and execution, surpassing strong baselines by a significant margin of +10.8% in end-to-end execution success rate, while exhibiting exceptional robustness and generalization capabilities.
title_zh: ToolOmni：通过主动检索与具身执行的代理学习实现开放世界工具使用
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）通过利用外部工具来提升其问题求解能力。然而，在工具库庞大且持续演化的开放世界场景中，现有依赖静态嵌入检索或工具参数记忆的方法，难以准确对齐用户意图与工具语义，或在面对未见过的工具时缺乏泛化能力，导致开放世界工具检索与执行的准确性不理想。为解决这些问题，我们提出了ToolOmni——一个统一的智能体框架，使LLM能够在推理循环中主动检索并基于现实依据执行开放世界的工具。首先，我们构建了一个冷启动多轮交互数据集，通过监督微调（SFT）赋予模型基础的智能体能力。随后，我们提出一种基于解耦多目标GRPO算法的开放世界工具学习方法，该方法在在线环境中同时优化LLM在工具检索准确率和执行有效性方面的表现。大量实验表明，ToolOmni在检索与执行两方面均达到当前最优性能，端到端执行成功率相比强基线显著提升10.8%，同时展现出卓越的鲁棒性与泛化能力。

title: RealVuln: Benchmarking Rule-Based, General-Purpose LLM, and Security-Specialized Scanners on Real-World Code
authors: John Pellew, Faizan Raza
abstract: How do security scanners perform on real-world code? We present RealVuln, the first open-source benchmark comparing Rule-Based SAST, General-Purpose LLMs, and Security-Specialized scanners on 26 intentionally vulnerable Python repositories (educational and Capture-The-Flag applications) with 796 hand-labeled entries (676 vulnerabilities, 120 false-positive traps). We test 15 scanners (3 Rule-Based SAST, 10 General-Purpose LLM, 2 Security-Specialized) and rank them by F3 score (beta=3, weighting recall 9x over precision). A clear three-tier ranking emerges under all metrics. Under F3, the Security-Specialized scanner Kolega.Dev (73.0) leads, followed by the best General-Purpose LLM, Claude Sonnet 4.6 (51.7), which in turn scores nearly 3x higher than the best Rule-Based tool, Semgrep (17.7). Under F1, Sonnet 4.6 leads (60.9) with Kolega.Dev at 52.4. Rankings within tiers shift with beta, but the three-tier hierarchy holds across all weightings. All code, ground-truth data, scanner outputs, and scoring scripts are released under an open-source license. An interactive dashboard is at https://realvuln.kolega.dev/. RealVuln is a living benchmark: versioned, community-driven, with a roadmap toward multi-language coverage.
title_zh: RealVuln：在真实代码上对基于规则、通用型大模型及安全专用扫描工具的基准测试
abstract_zh: 安全扫描器在真实代码上的表现如何？我们提出了 RealVuln——首个开源基准测试，用于对比基于规则的 SAST、通用大语言模型（LLM）以及专用安全扫描器在 26 个故意包含漏洞的 Python 项目（包括教育类和夺旗赛应用）上的表现。这些项目共包含 796 个手工标注的条目（其中 676 个为真实漏洞，120 个为误报陷阱）。我们测试了 15 款扫描器（3 款基于规则的 SAST，10 款通用大语言模型，2 款专用安全扫描器），并依据 F3 分数（β=3，召回率权重是精确率的 9 倍）进行排名。所有指标下均呈现出清晰的三层次排名结构。在 F3 指标下，专用安全扫描器 Kolega.Dev 以 73.0 分位居第一，其次是表现最佳的通用大语言模型 Claude Sonnet 4.6（51.7 分），其得分接近最佳基于规则工具 Semgrep（17.7 分）的三倍。在 F1 指标下，Sonnet 4.6 以 60.9 分领先，Kolega.Dev 位列第二，得分为 52.4。尽管不同 β 值下各层级内的排名会有所变化，但三层次的整体结构在所有权重设置下均保持稳定。所有代码、真实标签数据、扫描器输出结果及评分脚本均已开放源代码发布。交互式仪表板可访问 https://realvuln.kolega.dev/。RealVuln 是一个持续演进的基准：采用版本化管理，由社区驱动，并规划逐步扩展至多语言支持。

title: On the Effectiveness of Context Compression for Repository-Level Tasks: An Empirical Investigation
authors: Jia Feng, Zhanyue Qin, Cuiyun Gao, Ruiqi Wang, Chaozheng Wang, Yingwei Ma, Xiaoyuan Xie
abstract: Repository-level code intelligence tasks require large language models (LLMs) to process long, multi-file contexts. Such inputs introduce three challenges: crucial context can be obscured by noise, truncated due to limited windows, and increased inference latency. Context compression mitigates these risks by condensing inputs. While studied in NLP, its applicability to code tasks remains largely unexplored. We present the first systematic empirical study of context compression for repository-level code intelligence, organizing eight methods into three paradigms: discrete token sequences, continuous latent vectors, and visual tokens. We evaluate them on code completion and generation, measuring performance and efficiency. Results show context compression is effective: at 4x compression, continuous latent vector methods surpass full-context performance by up to 28.3% in BLEU score, indicating they filter noise rather than just truncating. On efficiency, all paradigms reduce inference cost. Both visual and text-based compression achieve up to 50% reduction in end-to-end latency at high ratios, approaching the cost of inference without repository context. These findings establish context compression as a viable approach and provide guidance for paradigm selection.
title_zh: 上下文压缩在仓库级任务中的有效性：一项实证研究
abstract_zh: 仓库级代码智能任务需要大语言模型（LLMs）处理长篇、多文件的上下文。这类输入带来了三大挑战：关键信息可能被噪声掩盖，因窗口限制而被截断，以及推理延迟增加。上下文压缩通过精简输入内容来缓解这些风险。尽管在自然语言处理领域已有研究，但其在代码任务中的适用性仍基本未被探索。本文首次系统地对仓库级代码智能中的上下文压缩进行了实证研究，将八种方法归纳为三种范式：离散的标记序列、连续的潜在向量和视觉标记。我们在代码补全与生成任务上评估了这些方法的性能与效率。结果表明，上下文压缩是有效的：在4倍压缩率下，连续潜在向量方法的BLEU得分比完整上下文表现最高提升28.3%，说明其不仅截断信息，更有效过滤噪声。在效率方面，所有范式均降低了推理成本。无论是视觉还是文本压缩，在高压缩比下均可实现高达50%的端到端延迟降低，接近于不使用仓库上下文时的推理开销。这些发现确立了上下文压缩作为一种可行方案的地位，并为不同范式的选取提供了指导。

title: WebMAC: A Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework for Scenario Testing of Web Systems
authors: Zhenyu Wan, Gong Chen, Qing Huang, Xiaoyuan Xie
abstract: Scenario testing is an important technique for detecting errors in web systems. Testers draft test scenarios and convert them into test scripts for execution. Early methods relied on testers to convert test scenarios into test scripts. Recent LLM-based scenario testing methods can generate test scripts from natural language descriptions of test scenarios. However, these methods are not only limited by the incompleteness of descriptions but also overlook test adequacy criteria, making it difficult to detect potential errors. To address these limitations, this paper proposes WebMAC, a multi-agent collaborative framework for scenario testing of web systems. WebMAC can complete natural language descriptions of test scenarios through interactive clarification and transform adequate instantiated test scenarios via equivalence class partitioning. WebMAC consists of three multi-agent modules, responsible respectively for completing natural language descriptions of test scenarios, transforming test scenarios, and converting test scripts. We evaluated WebMAC on four web systems. Compared with the SOTA method, WebMAC improves the execution success rate of generated test scripts by 30%-60%, increases testing efficiency by 29%, and reduces token consumption by 47.6%. Furthermore, WebMAC can effectively detect more errors in web systems.
title_zh: WebMAC：一种面向网络系统场景测试的多智能体协作框架
abstract_zh: 场景测试是检测Web系统错误的重要技术。测试人员会设计测试场景，并将其转换为可执行的测试脚本。早期的方法依赖测试人员手动将测试场景转化为测试脚本。近年来，基于大语言模型（LLM）的场景测试方法能够从自然语言描述的测试场景中自动生成测试脚本。然而，这些方法不仅受限于描述的不完整性，还忽视了测试充分性标准，难以有效发现潜在错误。为解决上述局限性，本文提出了一种名为WebMAC的多智能体协作框架，用于Web系统的场景测试。WebMAC通过交互式澄清来完善自然语言描述的测试场景，并利用等价类划分方法生成充分的实例化测试场景。WebMAC由三个多智能体模块组成，分别负责完善测试场景的自然语言描述、转换测试场景以及生成测试脚本。我们在四个Web系统上对WebMAC进行了评估。与当前最优方法相比，WebMAC使生成测试脚本的执行成功率提升了30%–60%，测试效率提高了29%，同时减少了47.6%的token消耗。此外，WebMAC还能更有效地发现Web系统中的更多错误。

title: Debugging Performance Issues in WebAssembly Runtimes via Mutation-based Inference
authors: Ruiying Zeng, Shuyao Jiang, Wenxuan Zhao, Yangfan Zhou
abstract: Performance debugging in WebAssembly (Wasm) runtimes is essential for ensuring the robustness of Wasm, especially since performance issues have frequently occurred in Wasm runtimes, which can significantly degrade the capabilities of hosted services. Many performance issues in Wasm runtimes result from suboptimal compilation of input Wasm programs, for which existing performance debugging methods primarily designed for application-level inefficiencies are not well-suited. In this paper, we present WarpL, a novel mutation-based approach that aims to identify the exact suboptimal instruction sequences responsible for the performance issues in Wasm runtimes, thereby narrowing down the root causes. Specifically, WarpL obtains a functionally similar mutant in which the performance issue does not manifest, and isolates the exact suboptimal instructions by comparing the machine code of the original and mutated programs. We implement WarpL as an open-source tool and evaluate it on 12 real-world performance issues across three widely used Wasm runtimes. WarpL identified the exact causes in 10 out of 12 issues. Notably, we have used WarpL to successfully diagnose six previously unknown performance issues in Wasmtime.
title_zh: 通过基于变异的推理调试WebAssembly运行时的性能问题
abstract_zh: WebAssembly（Wasm）运行时的性能调试对于保障Wasm的可靠性至关重要，尤其是因为Wasm运行时中频繁出现性能问题，这些问題会显著降低托管服务的能力。许多Wasm运行时中的性能问题源于输入Wasm程序编译不佳，而现有的性能调试方法主要针对应用层效率低下，难以有效应对这类问题。本文提出了一种名为WarpL的新颖突变式方法，旨在精准定位导致Wasm运行时性能问题的低效指令序列，从而缩小根本原因的范围。具体而言，WarpL生成一个功能上相似但性能问题不再显现的变异程序，并通过对比原程序与变异程序的机器码，精确识别出其中的低效指令。我们已将WarpL实现为开源工具，并在三个广泛使用的Wasm运行时中对12个真实世界中的性能问题进行了评估。实验结果表明，WarpL成功定位了12个问题中的10个根本原因。尤为值得一提的是，我们利用WarpL成功诊断出Wasmtime中六个此前未知的性能问题。

title: SafeHarness: Lifecycle-Integrated Security Architecture for LLM-based Agent Deployment
authors: Xixun Lin, Yang Liu, Yancheng Chen, Yongxuan Wu, Yucheng Ning, Yilong Liu, Nan Sun, Shun Zhang, Bin Chong, Chuan Zhou, Yanan Cao, Li Guo
abstract: The performance of large language model (LLM) agents depends critically on the execution harness, the system layer that orchestrates tool use, context management, and state persistence. Yet this same architectural centrality makes the harness a high-value attack surface: a single compromise at the harness level can cascade through the entire execution pipeline. We observe that existing security approaches suffer from structural mismatch, leaving them blind to harness-internal state and unable to coordinate across the different phases of agent operation. In this paper, we introduce \safeharness{}, a security architecture in which four proposed defense layers are woven directly into the agent lifecycle to address above significant limitations: adversarial context filtering at input processing, tiered causal verification at decision making, privilege-separated tool control at action execution, and safe rollback with adaptive degradation at state update. The proposed cross-layer mechanisms tie these layers together, escalating verification rigor, triggering rollbacks, and tightening tool privileges whenever sustained anomalies are detected. We evaluate \safeharness{} on benchmark datasets across diverse harness configurations, comparing against four security baselines under five attack scenarios spanning six threat categories. Compared to the unprotected baseline, \safeharness{} achieves an average reduction of approximately 38\% in UBR and 42\% in ASR, substantially lowering both the unsafe behavior rate and the attack success rate while preserving core task utility.
title_zh: SafeHarness：面向大语言模型智能体部署的生命周期集成安全架构
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）代理的性能在很大程度上依赖于执行环境（execution harness），即负责协调工具使用、上下文管理与状态持久化的系统层。然而，正是由于该架构的核心地位，执行环境也成为极具价值的攻击面：一旦在该层级遭到破坏，攻击便可能在整个执行流程中持续蔓延。我们观察到，现有的安全方法存在结构性不匹配问题，导致其无法感知执行环境内部的状态变化，也无法在代理运行的不同阶段之间实现有效协同。本文提出 \safeharness{}，一种全新的安全架构，通过将四个新设计的防御层直接嵌入代理生命周期中，以解决上述关键缺陷：在输入处理阶段实施对抗性上下文过滤，在决策阶段采用分层因果验证，在动作执行阶段实行权限分离的工具控制，以及在状态更新阶段引入安全回滚与自适应降级机制。所提出的跨层协同机制将各防御层紧密连接，一旦检测到持续异常，便自动提升验证强度、触发回滚操作，并收紧工具权限。我们在多种执行环境配置下的基准数据集上对 \safeharness{} 进行评估，对比了四种安全基线，在涵盖六类威胁的五种攻击场景下进行测试。与未受保护的基线相比，\safeharness{} 在平均无害行为率（UBR）上降低了约38%，在攻击成功率（ASR）上降低了42%，显著降低了不安全行为发生率和攻击成功率，同时保持了核心任务功能的可用性。

title: AgentComm: Semantic Communication for Embodied Agents
authors: Peiwen Jiang, Yushuo Feng, Jiajia Guo, Chao-Kai Wen, Shi Jin
abstract: The increasing deployment of agentic artificial intelligence (AI) systems has intensified the demand for efficient agent to agent communication, particularly over bandwidth limited wireless links. In embodied AI applications, agents must exchange task related information under strict latency and reliability constraints. Existing agent communication methods primarily focus on connectivity and protocol efficiency, but lack effective mechanisms to reduce physical layer transmission overhead while preserving task semantics.To address this challenge, this paper proposes a semantic agent communication framework that reduces communication overhead while maintaining task performance and shared understanding among agents. An LLM based semantic processor is first introduced to reorganize and condense agent generated messages by extracting task relevant semantic content. To cope with information loss introduced by aggressive message reduction, an importance-aware semantic transmission strategy is developed, which adaptively protects semantic components according to their task importance. Furthermore, a task specific knowledge base is incorporated as long term semantic memory to support recurring tasks and further reduce bandwidth consumption with minimal performance degradation. Experimental results and ablation studies demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves nearly 50% bandwidth reduction with negligible loss in task completion performance compared to conventional transmission schemes.
title_zh: AgentComm：面向具身智能体的语义通信
abstract_zh: 随着代理型人工智能（AI）系统部署的日益增多，对高效代理间通信的需求愈发强烈，尤其是在带宽受限的无线链路中。在具身AI应用中，代理必须在严格的延迟和可靠性约束下交换与任务相关的信息。现有的代理通信方法主要关注连接性和协议效率，但缺乏在保持任务语义的前提下有效降低物理层传输开销的机制。为应对这一挑战，本文提出了一种语义代理通信框架，在维持任务性能及代理间共享理解的同时，显著降低通信开销。该框架首先引入基于大语言模型（LLM）的语义处理器，通过提取与任务相关的语义内容，对代理生成的消息进行重新组织与压缩。为缓解因激进的消息压缩所导致的信息损失，本文进一步设计了一种重要性感知的语义传输策略，能够根据语义成分的任务重要性自适应地加以保护。此外，引入一个面向特定任务的知识库作为长期语义记忆，以支持重复性任务，并在几乎不降低性能的前提下进一步减少带宽消耗。实验结果与消融研究证明，相较于传统传输方案，所提出的框架实现了近50%的带宽节省，同时任务完成性能几乎无损。

title: Learned or Memorized ? Quantifying Memorization Advantage in Code LLMs
authors: Djiré Albérick Euraste, Kaboré Abdoul Kader, Jordan Samhi, Earl T. Barr, Jacques Klein, Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
abstract: The lack of transparency about code datasets used to train large language models (LLMs) makes it difficult to detect, evaluate, and mitigate data leakage. We present a perturbation-based method to quantify memorization advantage in code LLMs, defined as the performance gap between likely seen and unseen inputs. We evaluate 8 open-source code LLMs on 19 benchmarks across four task families: code generation, code understanding, vulnerability detection, and bug fixing. Sensitivity patterns vary widely across models and tasks. For example, StarCoder reaches high sensitivity on some benchmarks (up to 0.8), while QwenCoder remains lower (mostly below 0.4), suggesting differences in generalization behavior. Task categories also differ: code summarization tends to show low sensitivity, whereas test generation is substantially higher. We then analyze two widely discussed benchmarks, CVEFixes and Defects4J, often suspected of leakage. Contrary to common concerns, both show low memorization advantage across models: CVEFixes remains below 0.1, and Defects4J is lower than other program repair benchmarks. These results suggest that, for these datasets, models may rely more on learned generalization than direct memorization. Overall, our findings provide evidence that memorization risk is highly task- and model-dependent, and highlight the need for stronger evaluation protocols, especially in security-focused settings.
title_zh: 学习还是记忆？量化代码大模型中的记忆优势
abstract_zh: 由于大型语言模型（LLMs）所使用的代码数据集缺乏透明度，导致难以检测、评估和缓解数据泄露问题。本文提出一种基于扰动的方法，用于量化代码类大模型中的记忆优势，即模型在“可能见过”与“未见过”输入之间的性能差距。我们对8个开源代码LLM在19个基准测试上进行了评估，涵盖四大任务类别：代码生成、代码理解、漏洞检测和缺陷修复。不同模型和任务间的敏感性模式差异显著。例如，StarCoder在某些基准上的敏感性较高（最高达0.8），而QwenCoder则保持较低水平（大多低于0.4），反映出两者在泛化行为上的差异。任务类型也表现出明显区别：代码摘要任务通常敏感性较低，而测试用例生成任务的敏感性则显著更高。随后，我们分析了两个常被质疑存在数据泄露问题的基准——CVEFixes和Defects4J。与普遍担忧相反，这两个基准在各模型中均表现出较低的记忆优势：CVEFixes的值始终低于0.1，Defects4J也低于其他程序修复类基准。这些结果表明，对于这些数据集，模型更可能依赖于学习到的泛化能力，而非直接记忆。总体而言，我们的研究发现表明，记忆风险具有高度的任务和模型依赖性，强调了在安全相关场景中建立更严格评估协议的必要性。

title: How Can We Synthesize High-Quality Pretraining Data? A Systematic Study of Prompt Design, Generator Model, and Source Data
authors: Joel Niklaus, Atsuki Yamaguchi, Michal Štefánik, Guilherme Penedo, Hynek Kydlíček, Elie Bakouch, Lewis Tunstall, Edward Emanuel Beeching, Thibaud Frere, Colin Raffel, Leandro von Werra, Thomas Wolf
abstract: Synthetic data is a standard component in training large language models, yet systematic comparisons across design dimensions, including rephrasing strategy, generator model, and source data, remain absent. We conduct extensive controlled experiments, generating over one trillion tokens, to identify critical factors in rephrasing web text into synthetic pretraining data. Our results reveal that structured output formats, such as tables, math problems, FAQs, and tutorials, consistently outperform both curated web baselines and prior synthetic methods. Notably, increasing the size of the generator model beyond 1B parameters provides no additional benefit. Our analysis also demonstrates that the selection of the original data used for mixing substantially influences performance. By applying our findings, we develop \textbf{\textsc{FinePhrase}}, a 486-billion-token open dataset of rephrased web text. We show that \textsc{FinePhrase} outperforms all existing synthetic data baselines while reducing generation costs by up to 30 times. We provide the dataset, all prompts, and the generation framework to the research community.
title_zh: 如何合成高质量的预训练数据？关于提示设计、生成模型和源数据的系统性研究
abstract_zh: 合成数据是训练大型语言模型的标准组成部分，然而在重述策略、生成模型和源数据等设计维度之间，系统性的比较仍然缺失。我们开展了大规模的受控实验，生成了超过一万亿个标记（tokens），以识别将网络文本重述为合成预训练数据的关键因素。结果表明，结构化输出格式（如表格、数学问题、常见问题解答和教程）始终优于经过筛选的网络基线以及以往的合成方法。值得注意的是，将生成模型的规模扩大到10亿参数以上，并不会带来额外收益。我们的分析还表明，用于混合的原始数据选择对性能有显著影响。基于这些发现，我们开发了**\textbf{\textsc{FinePhrase}}**——一个包含4860亿标记的开放数据集，该数据集由重述后的网络文本构成。我们证明，\textsc{FinePhrase} 在所有现有合成数据基线上表现更优，同时将生成成本降低了多达30倍。我们已向研究社区公开该数据集、所有提示（prompts）以及生成框架。

title: ToolSpec: Accelerating Tool Calling via Schema-Aware and Retrieval-Augmented Speculative Decoding
authors: Heming Xia, Yongqi Li, Cunxiao Du, Mingbo Song, Wenjie Li
abstract: Tool calling has greatly expanded the practical utility of large language models (LLMs) by enabling them to interact with external applications. As LLM capabilities advance, effective tool use increasingly involves multi-step, multi-turn interactions to solve complex tasks. However, the resulting growth in tool interactions incurs substantial latency, posing a key challenge for real-time LLM serving. Through empirical analysis, we find that tool-calling traces are highly structured, conform to constrained schemas, and often exhibit recurring invocation patterns. Motivated by this, we propose ToolSpec, a schema-aware, retrieval-augmented speculative decoding method for accelerating tool calling. ToolSpec exploits predefined tool schemas to generate accurate drafts, using a finite-state machine to alternate between deterministic schema token filling and speculative generation for variable fields. In addition, ToolSpec retrieves similar historical tool invocations and reuses them as drafts to further improve efficiency. ToolSpec presents a plug-and-play solution that can be seamlessly integrated into existing LLM workflows. Experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that ToolSpec achieves up to a 4.2x speedup, substantially outperforming existing training-free speculative decoding methods.
title_zh: 工具规范：通过基于模式感知和检索增强的推测性解码加速工具调用
abstract_zh: 工具调用通过使大型语言模型（LLMs）能够与外部应用交互，极大地扩展了其实际应用价值。随着LLM能力的不断提升，高效使用工具越来越多地涉及多步骤、多轮次的交互以解决复杂任务。然而，随之而来的工具调用数量增长带来了显著的延迟问题，成为实时LLM服务面临的关键挑战。通过实证分析，我们发现工具调用轨迹具有高度结构化特征，遵循受限的模式，并常常表现出重复的调用模式。受此启发，我们提出了ToolSpec——一种面向模式感知、基于检索增强的推测性解码方法，用于加速工具调用。ToolSpec利用预定义的工具模式生成准确的草稿，通过有限状态机在确定性的模式字段填充与可变字段的推测性生成之间交替进行。此外，ToolSpec还会检索相似的历史工具调用记录，并将其复用为生成草稿，进一步提升效率。ToolSpec是一种即插即用的解决方案，可无缝集成到现有的LLM工作流中。在多个基准测试上的实验表明，ToolSpec实现了最高达4.2倍的加速效果，显著优于现有的无需训练的推测性解码方法。

title: Quantifying and Understanding Uncertainty in Large Reasoning Models
authors: Yangyi Li, Chenxu Zhao, Mengdi Huai
abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) have recently demonstrated significant improvements in complex reasoning. While quantifying generation uncertainty in LRMs is crucial, traditional methods are often insufficient because they do not provide finite-sample guarantees for reasoning-answer generation. Conformal prediction (CP) stands out as a distribution-free and model-agnostic methodology that constructs statistically rigorous uncertainty sets. However, existing CP methods ignore the logical connection between the reasoning trace and the final answer. Additionally, prior studies fail to interpret the origins of uncertainty coverage for LRMs as they typically overlook the specific training factors driving valid reasoning. Notably, it is challenging to disentangle reasoning quality from answer correctness when quantifying uncertainty, while simultaneously establishing theoretical guarantees for computationally efficient explanation methods. To address these challenges, we first propose a novel methodology that quantifies uncertainty in the reasoning-answer structure with statistical guarantees. Subsequently, we develop a unified example-to-step explanation framework using Shapley values that identifies a provably sufficient subset of training examples and their key reasoning steps to preserve the guarantees. We also provide theoretical analyses of our proposed methods. Extensive experiments on challenging reasoning datasets verify the effectiveness of the proposed methods.
title_zh: 大型推理模型中不确定性的量化与理解
abstract_zh: 大型推理模型（LRMs）最近在复杂推理任务上展现出显著的性能提升。尽管对LRM生成结果的不确定性进行量化至关重要，但传统方法往往不足，因为它们无法为推理-答案生成提供有限样本下的统计保证。置信预测（Conformal Prediction, CP）作为一种无需依赖分布假设且与模型无关的方法，能够构建具有严格统计意义的不确定性集合。然而，现有的CP方法忽略了推理过程与最终答案之间的逻辑关联。此外，以往研究未能解释LRM不确定性覆盖的根源，通常忽视了驱动有效推理的具体训练因素。值得注意的是，在量化不确定性时，难以将推理质量与答案正确性区分开来，同时又要为计算高效的解释方法建立理论保障。针对这些挑战，我们首先提出一种新颖的方法，能够在推理-答案结构中实现具有统计保证的不确定性量化。随后，我们开发了一种统一的“示例到步骤”解释框架，利用Shapley值识别出一个可证明充分的训练示例及其关键推理步骤子集，以保持上述统计保证。我们还对所提方法进行了理论分析。在多个具有挑战性的推理数据集上的大量实验验证了所提方法的有效性。

title: Agentic Open RAN: A Deterministic and Auditable Framework for Intent-Driven Radio Control
authors: Hengxu Li, Dongkuan Xu, Mingzhe Chen, Yuchen Liu
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) open new possibilities for agentic control in Open RAN, allowing operators to express intents in natural language while delegating low-level execution to autonomous agents. We present A1gent, an agentic RAN control stack that decouples reasoning from real-time actuation. A non-RT agentic rApp compiles operator goals into typed A1 policy instances, and three task-oriented near-RT agentic xApps enforce them through a deterministic loop with plane-scoped actuation - E2 for mobility and load steering, and O1 for energy orchestration. This agentic reasoning-execution split ensures auditable coordination between RAN intelligent controller (RIC) tiers, supported by encoded guardrails and a fixed-priority action merger for conflict governance. A training-free adaptive policy tuner then refines bounded parameters using KPI memory without retraining, sustaining predictable adaptation. By integrating intent-driven planning with deterministic near-RT execution, A1gent advances Open RAN toward verifiable, self-governing, and reproducible agentic intelligence.
title_zh: 智能开放无线接入网：一种可确定性和可审计的意图驱动无线控制框架
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）为开放无线接入网（Open RAN）中的智能体控制带来了新的可能性，使运营商能够以自然语言表达意图，同时将底层执行任务交由自主智能体完成。我们提出A1gent——一种智能体化的RAN控制架构，其核心是将推理与实时动作执行解耦。非实时（non-RT）智能体应用（rApp）将运营商的目标编译为具有类型约束的A1策略实例，而三个面向任务的近实时（near-RT）智能体应用（xApps）则通过一个确定性的循环，在全局平面范围内执行动作：E2用于移动性和负载引导，O1用于能源调度。这种智能体推理-执行分离机制确保了RAN智能控制器（RIC）各层级之间可审计的协同运作，依托编码化的约束规则和固定优先级的动作合并机制实现冲突治理。此外，一个无需训练的自适应策略调优器通过利用关键性能指标（KPI）记忆来优化有界参数，无需重新训练即可实现可预测的动态适应。通过将意图驱动的规划与确定性的近实时执行相结合，A1gent推动Open RAN向可验证、自我管理且可复现的智能体化智能迈进。

title: Neural architectures for resolving references in program code
authors: Gergő Szalay, Gergely Zsolt Kovács, Sándor Teleki, Balázs Pintér, Tibor Gregorics
abstract: Resolving and rewriting references is fundamental in programming languages. Motivated by a real-world decompilation task, we abstract reference rewriting into the problems of direct and indirect indexing by permutation. We create synthetic benchmarks for these tasks and show that well-known sequence-to-sequence machine learning architectures are struggling on these benchmarks. We introduce new sequence-to-sequence architectures for both problems. Our measurements show that our architectures outperform the baselines in both robustness and scalability: our models can handle examples that are ten times longer compared to the best baseline. We measure the impact of our architecture in the real-world task of decompiling switch statements, which has an indexing subtask. According to our measurements, the extended model decreases the error rate by 42%. Multiple ablation studies show that all components of our architectures are essential.
title_zh: 程序代码中指代消解的神经架构
abstract_zh: 在编程语言中，解决和重写引用是基础性任务。受一个真实世界的反编译任务启发，我们将引用重写抽象为通过置换实现的直接索引和间接索引问题。我们为此类任务创建了合成基准测试，并发现现有的知名序列到序列机器学习架构在这些基准上表现不佳。为此，我们提出了针对这两个问题的新序列到序列架构。我们的实验结果表明，与基线方法相比，我们的架构在鲁棒性和可扩展性方面均有显著提升：我们的模型能够处理长度是最佳基线模型十倍的示例。我们还评估了该架构在真实世界反编译任务中的实际影响，特别是处理包含索引子任务的switch语句。根据测量结果，改进后的模型将错误率降低了42%。多项消融实验表明，我们架构中的所有组件均至关重要。

title: Empirical Evidence of Complexity-Induced Limits in Large Language Models on Finite Discrete State-Space Problems with Explicit Validity Constraints
authors: Md. Fahad Ullah Utsho, Mohd. Ruhul Ameen, Akif Islam, Md. Golam Rashed, Dipankar Das
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly described as possessing strong reasoning capabilities, supported by high performance on mathematical, logical, and planning benchmarks. However, most existing evaluations rely on aggregate accuracy over fixed datasets, obscuring how reasoning behavior evolves as task complexity increases. In this work, we introduce a controlled benchmarking framework to systematically evaluate the robustness of reasoning in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) under progressively increasing problem complexity. We construct a suite of nine classical reasoning tasks: Boolean Satisfiability, Cryptarithmetic, Graph Coloring, River Crossing, Tower of Hanoi, Water Jug, Checker Jumping, Sudoku, and Rubik's Cube, each parameterized to precisely control complexity while preserving underlying semantics. Using deterministic validators, we evaluate multiple open and proprietary LRMs across low, intermediate, and high complexity regimes, ensuring that only fully valid solutions are accepted. Our results reveal a consistent phase transition like behavior: models achieve high accuracy at low complexity but degrade sharply beyond task specific complexity thresholds. We formalize this phenomenon as reasoning collapse. Across tasks, we observe substantial accuracy declines, often exceeding 50%, accompanied by inconsistent reasoning traces, constraint violations, loss of state tracking, and confidently incorrect outputs. Increased reasoning length does not reliably improve correctness, and gains in one problem family do not generalize to others. These findings highlight the need for evaluation methodologies that move beyond static benchmarks and explicitly measure reasoning robustness under controlled complexity.
title_zh: 大规模语言模型在具有显式有效性约束的有限离散状态空间问题中复杂性导致的局限性的实证证据
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLMs）正被越来越多地描述为具备强大的推理能力，这一观点得到了其在数学、逻辑和规划类基准测试中优异表现的支持。然而，现有的大多数评估方法依赖于固定数据集上的整体准确率，这掩盖了随着任务复杂度提升时推理行为的演变过程。在本研究中，我们提出了一种受控的基准评估框架，以系统性地考察大型推理模型（LRMs）在问题复杂度逐步增加情况下的推理鲁棒性。我们构建了九个经典的推理任务：布尔可满足性、密码算术、图着色、河岸过桥、汉诺塔、水壶问题、跳棋游戏、数独以及魔方，每个任务均通过参数化设计精确控制复杂度，同时保持其内在语义不变。借助确定性验证器，我们在低、中、高三个复杂度层级上对多个开源与专有LRM进行了评估，确保仅接受完全正确的解。

我们的结果揭示出一种类似相变的行为模式：在低复杂度下，模型表现出高准确率，但一旦超过特定任务的复杂度阈值，性能便急剧下降。我们将这一现象形式化为“推理崩溃”（reasoning collapse）。在各类任务中，我们观察到显著的准确率下降，降幅常超过50%，并伴随推理轨迹不一致、约束违反、状态追踪丢失以及自信却错误的输出等问题。推理长度的增加并不能可靠提升正确性，且某一问题类别中的改进也无法泛化至其他问题。这些发现凸显了现有评估方法的局限性，强调必须超越静态基准，发展能够明确衡量推理鲁棒性的新评估范式，尤其是在可控复杂度条件下的动态评估。

title: KindHML: formal verification of smart contracts based on Hennessy-Milner logic
authors: Massimo Bartoletti, Angelo Ferrando, Enrico Lipparini, Vadim Malvone
abstract: Smart contracts deployed on blockchains such as Ethereum routinely manage large amounts of assets, making their security critical. Empirical studies show that real-world attacks often exploit flaws in the business logic of contracts that unfold across multiple transactions, such as liquidity or front-running attacks. Detecting these attacks requires reasoning about expressive temporal properties beyond the capabilities of existing analysis tools. In this paper, we present an automated approach to the formal verification of smart contracts, enabling the specification and verification of complex temporal properties. Our approach provides a fully automated encoding into Lustre -- the specification language supported by the Kind 2 model checker -- of an expressive subset of Solidity contracts and temporal specifications based on first-order Hennessy-Milner Logic. This encoding allows us to leverage Kind 2 to determine whether the contract respects the specification or not. We implement our approach in a toolchain that integrates the translation and verification steps, and we evaluate its effectiveness and performance on a benchmark of smart contracts and temporal properties capturing complex attack scenarios. Our results show that the proposed approach can effectively verify non-trivial temporal properties of smart contracts and detect violations that are beyond the reach of existing analysis tools.
title_zh: KindHML：基于Hennessy-Milner逻辑的智能合约形式化验证
abstract_zh: 在以太坊等区块链上部署的智能合约经常管理大量资产，因此其安全性至关重要。实证研究表明，现实世界中的攻击往往利用跨多个交易展开的合约业务逻辑缺陷，例如流动性攻击或抢先交易（front-running）攻击。检测此类攻击需要对超越现有分析工具能力的复杂时序属性进行推理。本文提出一种智能合约形式化验证的自动化方法，能够对复杂的时序属性进行规范描述与验证。我们的方法将Solidity合约的一个表达性子集以及基于一阶Hennessy-Milner逻辑的时序规范，完全自动化地编码为Lustre语言——该语言是Kind 2模型检查器所支持的规范语言。通过这一编码，我们可借助Kind 2判断合约是否满足给定规范。我们实现了一个集成翻译与验证步骤的工具链，并在包含复杂攻击场景的智能合约与时序属性基准测试中评估了该方法的有效性与性能。实验结果表明，所提出的方案能够有效验证智能合约的非平凡时序属性，并发现现有分析工具无法触及的违规行为。

title: Fully Dynamic Maintenance of Loop Nesting Forests in Reducible Flow Graphs
authors: Gregory Morse, Tamás Kozsik
abstract: Loop nesting forests (LNFs) are a fundamental abstraction for reasoning about control-flow structure, enabling applications such as compiler optimizations, program analysis, and dominator computation. While efficient static algorithms for constructing LNFs are well understood, maintaining them under dynamic graph updates has remained largely unexplored due to the lack of efficient dynamic depth-first search (DFS) maintenance. In this paper, we present the first fully dynamic algorithm for maintaining loop nesting forests in reducible control-flow graphs. Our approach leverages recent advances in dynamic DFS maintenance to incrementally update loop structure under edge insertions and deletions. We show that updates can be confined to local regions of the depth-first spanning tree, avoiding global recomputation. We provide formal invariants, correctness arguments, and complexity analysis, and demonstrate how the maintained LNF enables efficient derivation of dominance information. Our results establish LNFs as a practical dynamic abstraction for modern compiler and analysis pipelines.
title_zh: 可约流图中循环嵌套森林的完全动态维护
abstract_zh: 循环嵌套森林（LNFs）是用于推理控制流结构的基本抽象，支持编译器优化、程序分析以及支配关系计算等应用。尽管构建LNF的高效静态算法已广为人知，但由于缺乏高效的动态深度优先搜索（DFS）维护机制，其在动态图更新下的维护问题长期以来未被充分探索。本文首次提出一种完全动态算法，用于在可约控制流图中维护循环嵌套森林。我们的方法利用近期在动态DFS维护方面的进展，能够在边的插入与删除操作下增量式地更新循环结构。我们证明，这些更新可被限制在深度优先生成树的局部区域，从而避免全局重新计算。本文提供了形式化的不变量、正确性论证及复杂度分析，并展示了所维护的LNF如何高效推导出支配信息。研究结果确立了LNF作为现代编译器与分析流水线中实用的动态抽象的可行性。

title: From Where Words Come: Efficient Regularization of Code Tokenizers Through Source Attribution
authors: Pavel Chizhov, Egor Bogomolov, Ivan P. Yamshchikov
abstract: Efficiency and safety of Large Language Models (LLMs), among other factors, rely on the quality of tokenization. A good tokenizer not only improves inference speed and language understanding but also provides extra defense against jailbreak attacks and lowers the risk of hallucinations. In this work, we investigate the efficiency of code tokenization, in particular from the perspective of data source diversity. We demonstrate that code tokenizers are prone to producing unused, and thus under-trained, tokens due to the imbalance in repository and language diversity in the training data, as well as the dominance of source-specific, repetitive tokens that are often unusable in future inference. By modifying the BPE objective and introducing merge skipping, we implement different techniques under the name Source-Attributed BPE (SA-BPE) to regularize BPE training and minimize overfitting, thereby substantially reducing the number of under-trained tokens while maintaining the same inference procedure as with regular BPE. This provides an effective tool suitable for production use.
title_zh: 词语的来源：通过源归属实现代码分词器的有效正则化
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的效率与安全性，以及其他诸多因素，都依赖于分词质量。一个优秀的分词器不仅能提升推理速度和语言理解能力，还能为抵御“越狱攻击”提供额外防护，并降低幻觉风险。在本研究中，我们从数据源多样性的角度出发，探讨了代码分词的效率问题。我们发现，由于训练数据中仓库和编程语言分布的不平衡，代码分词器容易生成大量未被使用、因而训练不足的词汇单元；同时，源代码特有的重复性词汇占主导地位，这些词汇在未来的推理过程中往往无法有效使用。为此，我们通过修改BPE（字节对编码）的目标函数，并引入合并跳过机制，提出了一系列名为“源属性BPE”（Source-Attributed BPE, SA-BPE）的技术，以规范BPE训练过程，有效减少过拟合现象。该方法显著降低了训练不足词汇的数量，同时保持与传统BPE相同的推理流程，从而提供了一种适用于生产环境的有效工具。

title: TIP: Token Importance in On-Policy Distillation
authors: Yuanda Xu, Hejian Sang, Zhengze Zhou, Ran He, Zhipeng Wang, Alborz Geramifard
abstract: On-policy knowledge distillation (OPD) trains a student on its own rollouts under token-level supervision from a teacher. Not all token positions matter equally, but existing views of token importance are incomplete. We ask a direct question: which tokens carry the most useful learning signal in OPD? Our answer is that informative tokens come from two regions: positions with high student entropy, and positions with low student entropy plus high teacher--student divergence, where the student is overconfident and wrong. Empirically, student entropy is a strong first-order proxy: retaining $50\%$ of tokens with entropy-based sampling matches or exceeds all-token training while reducing peak memory by up to $47\%$. But entropy alone misses a second important region. When we isolate low-entropy, high-divergence tokens, training on fewer than $10\%$ of all tokens nearly matches full-token baselines, showing that overconfident tokens carry dense corrective signal despite being nearly invisible to entropy-only rules. We organize these findings with TIP (Token Importance in on-Policy distillation), a two-axis taxonomy over student entropy and teacher--student divergence, and give a theoretical explanation for why entropy is useful yet structurally incomplete. This view motivates type-aware token selection rules that combine uncertainty and disagreement. We validate this picture across three teacher--student pairs spanning Qwen3, Llama, and Qwen2.5 on MATH-500 and AIME 2024/2025, and on the DeepPlanning benchmark for long-horizon agentic planning, where Q3-only training on $<$$20\%$ of tokens surpasses full-token OPD. Our experiments are implemented by extending the OPD repository https://github.com/HJSang/OPSD_OnPolicyDistillation, which supports memory-efficient distillation of larger models under limited GPU budgets.
title_zh: 提示：策略内蒸馏中的标记重要性
abstract_zh:
在策略知识蒸馏（OPD）中，学生模型基于自身生成的轨迹，在教师模型的逐标记监督下进行训练。并非所有标记位置都具有同等重要性，但现有的关于标记重要性的观点仍不完整。我们提出一个直接问题：在OPD中，哪些标记携带了最有价值的学习信号？我们的答案是，信息量丰富的标记主要来自两个区域：一是学生模型熵值较高的位置，二是学生模型熵值较低但与教师模型存在高差异的位置——即学生过于自信且出错的区域。

实证研究表明，学生模型的熵值是一个强有力的首阶代理指标：仅保留熵值最高的50%标记进行训练，即可达到甚至超越使用全部标记的训练效果，同时将峰值内存消耗降低高达47%。然而，仅依赖熵值会遗漏第二个关键区域。当我们单独提取低熵但高差异的标记时，仅用不到全部标记10%的数据进行训练，其性能已几乎完全匹配全标记基线，这表明那些“过度自信”的标记虽然在仅以熵值为依据的选择规则下几乎不可见，却蕴含着密集的纠正性信号。

为此，我们提出了TIP（On-Policy蒸馏中的标记重要性）框架，这是一个基于学生模型熵值与师生差异双重维度的二维分类体系，并从理论上解释了为何熵值虽有用但本质上存在结构性缺陷。这一视角启发了结合不确定性与分歧度的类型感知型标记选择策略。我们在三个不同的师生模型组合上验证了该观点，涵盖Qwen3、Llama和Qwen2.5，分别在MATH-500、AIME 2024/2025数据集以及DeepPlanning长程智能体规划基准测试上进行了实验。结果表明，在长程规划任务中，仅使用Q3阶段的少于20%的标记进行训练，其表现已超越传统的全标记OPD方法。

所有实验均通过扩展OPD开源仓库 https://github.com/HJSang/OPSD_OnPolicyDistillation 实现，该工具支持在有限GPU资源条件下高效蒸馏更大规模模型，显著提升知识蒸馏过程的内存效率。

title: The Cognitive Circuit Breaker: A Systems Engineering Framework for Intrinsic AI Reliability
authors: Jonathan Pan
abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in mission-critical software systems, detecting hallucinations and ``faked truthfulness'' has become a paramount engineering challenge. Current reliability architectures rely heavily on post-generation, black-box mechanisms, such as Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) cross-checking or LLM-as-a-judge evaluators. These extrinsic methods introduce unacceptable latency, high computational overhead, and reliance on secondary external API calls, frequently violating standard software engineering Service Level Agreements (SLAs). In this paper, we propose the Cognitive Circuit Breaker, a novel systems engineering framework that provides intrinsic reliability monitoring with minimal latency overhead. By extracting hidden states during a model's forward pass, we calculate the ``Cognitive Dissonance Delta'' -- the mathematical gap between an LLM's outward semantic confidence (softmax probabilities) and its internal latent certainty (derived via linear probes). We demonstrate statistically significant detection of cognitive dissonance, highlight architecture-dependent Out-of-Distribution (OOD) generalization, and show that this framework adds negligible computational overhead to the active inference pipeline.
title_zh: 认知断路器：一种内在人工智能可靠性的系统工程框架
abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型（LLMs）在关键任务软件系统中的日益部署，检测幻觉现象及“虚假真实性”已成为一项至关重要的工程挑战。当前的可靠性架构严重依赖于生成后的黑箱机制，例如基于检索增强生成（RAG）的交叉验证或以大模型作为评判者的评估器。这些外部方法引入了不可接受的延迟、高昂的计算开销，并且依赖于次级外部API调用，常常违反标准软件工程的服务水平协议（SLA）。本文提出了一种名为“认知熔断器”（Cognitive Circuit Breaker）的新颖系统工程框架，能够在极低延迟开销下实现内在的可靠性监控。通过在模型前向传播过程中提取隐藏状态，我们计算出“认知失调差值”——即大模型外在语义置信度（softmax概率）与其内部潜在确定性（通过线性探测推导得出）之间的数学差距。我们实证表明，该方法能够显著检测到认知失调现象，揭示了架构相关的分布外（OOD）泛化特性，并证明该框架对实际推理流程的计算开销几乎可以忽略不计。

title: Dataset-Level Metrics Attenuate Non-Determinism: A Fine-Grained Non-Determinism Evaluation in Diffusion Language Models
authors: Zhengyu Fang, Zhimeng Jiang, Huiyuan Chen, Xiaoge Zhang, Tianyi Li, Kaiyu Tang, Xiao Li, Jing Li
abstract: Diffusion language models (DLMs) have emerged as a promising paradigm for large language models (LLMs), yet the non-deterministic behavior of DLMs remains poorly understood. The existing non-determinism evaluations for LLMs predominantly rely on dataset-level metrics under fixed inference configurations, providing limited insight into how model behavior varies across runs and evaluation conditions. In this work, we show that dataset-level metrics systematically attenuate non-determinism in diffusion language models by aggregating sample-level prediction quality across different runs. As a result, configurations with similar aggregate performance can exhibit substantially different behaviors on individual inputs, leaving fine-grained instability and distinct error patterns uncharacterized. To address this limitation, we conduct a fine-grained evaluation of non-determinism based on sample-level prediction differences across a range of model-related factors-including guidance scale, diffusion steps, and Monte Carlo sampling-as well as system-related factors such as batch size, hardware, and numerical precision. Our analysis reveals that non-determinism in DLMs is pervasive and structured, with code generation exhibiting markedly higher sensitivity to factor-level choices than question answering. To attribute sources of non-determinism evaluation, we introduce Factor Variance Attribution (FVA), a cross-factor analysis metric that decomposes observed non-determinism into variance attributable to different evaluation factor settings. Our findings highlight the need for fine-grained, factor-aware evaluation to enable reliable non-determinism assessment of diffusion language models.
title_zh: 数据集级度量减弱非确定性：扩散语言模型中细粒度的非确定性评估
abstract_zh: 扩散语言模型（DLMs）作为一种新兴的大型语言模型（LLMs）范式，展现出广阔前景，但其非确定性行为仍缺乏深入理解。目前针对大模型非确定性的评估大多依赖于固定推理配置下的数据集级指标，难以揭示模型在不同运行和评估条件下行为的细微变化。本文表明，数据集级指标会系统性地弱化扩散语言模型中的非确定性表现，因为它们将不同运行中样本级别的预测质量进行聚合，导致看似相似的总体性能下，单个输入的表现可能截然不同，从而掩盖了细粒度的不稳定性及独特的错误模式。为克服这一局限，我们基于样本级别的预测差异，开展了一项细粒度的非确定性评估，考察多种与模型相关的因素（如引导尺度、扩散步数、蒙特卡洛采样）以及系统相关因素（如批处理大小、硬件平台、数值精度）的影响。分析结果表明，DLMs中的非确定性普遍存在且具有结构性特征，其中代码生成任务对各类因素的选择表现出远高于问答任务的敏感性。为进一步归因非确定性来源，我们提出一种名为“因子方差归因”（Factor Variance Attribution, FVA）的跨因子分析指标，能够将观测到的非确定性分解为由不同评估因子设置所引起的方差贡献。研究结果强调，必须采用细粒度、因子感知的评估方法，才能实现对扩散语言模型非确定性的可靠评估。

title: An Empirical Investigation of Practical LLM-as-a-Judge Improvement Techniques on RewardBench 2
authors: Ryan Lail
abstract: LLM-as-a-judge, using a language model to score or rank candidate responses, is widely used as a scalable alternative to human evaluation in RLHF pipelines, benchmarking, and application layer evaluations (evals). However, judgment reliability depends heavily on prompting and aggregation strategy. We present an empirical investigation of practical, drop-in techniques that improve GPT-5.4 judge accuracy on RewardBench 2 without any finetuning. Two techniques account for nearly all available gains: task-specific criteria injection (+3.0pp at negligible cost) and ensemble scoring (+9.8pp at 5x cost). Combined, they reach 83.6% accuracy, +11.9pp over the 71.7% baseline. Our investigation also covers three further techniques (calibration context, adaptive model escalation, and soft blending) which did not reliably improve on criteria + ensembling at comparable cost. Cheaper model tiers benefit disproportionately from ensembling: GPT-5.4 mini with k=8 achieves 79.2% at 1.2x baseline cost, and GPT-5.4 nano with k=8 reaches 71.4% at 0.4x baseline cost, making high-accuracy LLM judges accessible at low cost.
title_zh: 基于RewardBench 2的大型语言模型作为裁判的实用改进技术实证研究
abstract_zh: 作为评判者的大型语言模型（LLM-as-a-judge），即利用语言模型对候选回答进行评分或排序，已成为强化学习人类反馈（RLHF）流程、基准测试以及应用层评估中一种可扩展的替代人工评价的方法。然而，其判断的可靠性在很大程度上取决于提示设计和结果聚合策略。本文通过实证研究，提出了一系列即插即用的实用技术，在无需微调的情况下显著提升了GPT-5.4在RewardBench 2上的判别准确率。其中两种技术贡献了几乎全部性能提升：特定任务标准注入（仅需极低成本，提升3.0个百分点）和集成评分（提升9.8个百分点，代价为5倍），二者结合后达到83.6%的准确率，相较71.7%的基线水平提升了11.9个百分点。此外，我们还考察了三种其他技术（校准上下文、自适应模型升级和软融合），但它们在相近成本下未能持续超越“标准注入+集成评分”的组合效果。值得注意的是，成本更低的模型层级从集成评分中获益更为显著：GPT-5.4 mini在k=8时达到79.2%准确率，仅需1.2倍基线成本；而GPT-5.4 nano在k=8时达到71.4%准确率，成本仅为基线的0.4倍，使得高精度的LLM评判者得以以极低成本实现。

title: Weight Patching: Toward Source-Level Mechanistic Localization in LLMs
authors: Chenghao Sun, Chengsheng Zhang, Guanzheng Qin, Rui Dai, Xinmei Tian
abstract: Mechanistic interpretability seeks to localize model behavior to the internal components that causally realize it.
